query:
does this make sense? Root Cause: RecurringFrictionRule implements ExtractionRule::evaluate() and writes ProposedEntry objects (category: lesson-learned, topic: process-improvement) directly to ENTRIES via            
  store.insert(). These entries pollute the knowledge base with low-quality process noise.
                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  Fix Plan:                                                                              
  1. recurring_friction.rs — Remove ExtractionRule impl, replace with compute_friction_recommendations() -> Vec<String> (pure signal extraction, no persistence)                                     
  2. extraction/mod.rs — Remove RecurringFrictionRule from default_extraction_rules() (4→3 rules)                                                               
  3. background.rs — Call compute_friction_recommendations inside extraction_tick()'s spawn_blocking closure, add friction_signals: Vec<String> to TickMetadata, add one-shot quarantine migration   
  for existing junk entries (COUNTERS-gated)                                                                                                                                                      
  4. mcp/tools.rs — Append friction_signals to maintenance_recommendations in context_status response (unconditional)                                                                                
                                                                                         
  New test: test_extraction_tick_does_not_write_recurring_friction_to_entries

entries.title: ExtractionRule vs maintenance action: additive rules must not signal deprecation (GH #351)
ADR-002 crt-018b: Hold (Not Increment) consecutive_bad_cycles on Background Tick Error
ExtractionRule must not perform side effects: ephemeral signals belong in compute functions inside spawn_blocking, not in ProposedEntry output
topic_signal write-time enrichment: session registry in-memory feature as fallback
ADR-004 col-027: Atomic Fix for Duplicated Pre-Post Differential in metrics.rs and friction.rs
